{"id":"8128823a-e372-42aa-9e64-acc0585ab0f1","identifier":"FRG-229","title":"Bug: Stale slot reservations — objects stay 'full' after character leaves","description":"## Problem

QA confirmed: objects show 'full' (occupied pip) permanently after characters finish interacting and leave. Stove showed 'full' for 8+ sim hours with no character present. Reproduced on stove, desk, easel, fridge, bonsai. Only Reset clears the stale slots.

## Evidence

* Stove: full from 2:40 PM onwards, no character at stove. Persisted until Reset.
* After Reset: Nyx used stove \~10:12 AM, stove shows full at 1:46 PM with no character present.
* Bonsai: showed full for hours, eventually freed inconsistently.

## Root Cause

The interaction-end detection in Engine.js (line 108) checks prevAction === 'interacting' && char.action !== 'interacting' && prevInteractTarget, then calls objectEngine.release(). This SHOULD work but QA shows it doesn't reliably fire.

Possible causes:

1. The prevAction/prevInteractTarget capture (line 67-68) and char.update() (line 105) have a race — if something else changes the action between capture and check
2. arrive() is called with [char.name](<http://char.name>) but release() might use a different identifier
3. \_handleObjectArrival clears targetObjectId before startInteraction — ordering issue with the detection
4. The object's auto_next timer might reset the state (and clear the interaction) BEFORE the character's interactTimer expires, breaking the detection

## Fix

Add defensive release: when a character transitions out of interacting (in Character.js or Engine.js), always release the slot. Add console.warn for any release on an unknown occupant to catch orphaned slots.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Objects return to correct free count after character finishes interaction
* No stale full pips after 10+ sim hours
* QA re-verification passes","projectMilestone":{"id":"d98e487a-66f2-4106-86d3-550aac4cb898","name":"M1: Sim engine correct (debug foundation)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-229/bug-stale-slot-reservations-objects-stay-full-after-character-leaves","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-229-bug-stale-slot-reservations-objects-stay-full-after","createdAt":"2026-03-07T22:12:41.381Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T22:12:41.381Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

Three distinct paths could leave an object permanently 'full' after its
character left:

   loop — if `interactTarget` is set but `action !== 'interacting'`, log
   a warning and release immediately.  This catches any future code path

## Summary

Fixes three distinct code paths that caused objects to stay permanently 'full' after a character finished interacting and left.

- **Async AI race (primary cause):** `_applyDecision` could resolve between animation frames while a character was `interacting`, calling `moveTo()` and changing `action` to `'walking'` before the next frame's pre-update capture. The `prevAction === 'interacting'` detection then missed the transition entirely and the occupancy slot was never released. Fixed by guarding `_applyDecision` to discard stale decisions when character is already in `'interacting'` or `'talking'` state.
- **Reservation leak via `_startConversation`:** When CharB was walking toward an object and became the target of a conversation, `startTalk()` cleared `targetObjectId` but never called `objectEngine.release()`, permanently orphaning the reservation in the object's `_reservations` set. Fixed by releasing CharB's pending reservation in `_startConversation` before entering the talking state.
- **Defensive safety net:** Added a check at the top of the character loop — if `interactTarget` is set but `action !== 'interacting'`, logs a warning and releases immediately. Catches any future path that changes `action` without going through the normal end-of-interaction detection.

Also adds `console.warn` in `ObjectInstance.release()` when the charName is not a known occupant or reservation, making any future orphaned slots visible in the debug console.

## Test plan

- [ ] Run sim for 10+ sim hours and verify no object remains full after its character walks away
- [ ] Verify stove, desk, easel, fridge, bonsai all return to available state after use
- [ ] Check browser console: no `[Engine] defensive release` warnings during normal gameplay (confirms the async guard is working preventively)
- [ ] Trigger a talk interruption mid-walk-to-object and verify the object slot is freed (no stale reservation)
- [ ] QA re-verification pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

{"id":"8128823a-e372-42aa-9e64-acc0585ab0f1","identifier":"FRG-229","title":"Bug: Stale slot reservations — objects stay 'full' after character leaves","description":"## Problem

QA confirmed: objects show 'full' (occupied pip) permanently after characters finish interacting and leave. Stove showed 'full' for 8+ sim hours with no character present. Reproduced on stove, desk, easel, fridge, bonsai. Only Reset clears the stale slots.

## Evidence

* Stove: full from 2:40 PM onwards, no character at stove. Persisted until Reset.
* After Reset: Nyx used stove \~10:12 AM, stove shows full at 1:46 PM with no character present.
* Bonsai: showed full for hours, eventually freed inconsistently.

## Root Cause

The interaction-end detection in Engine.js (line 108) checks prevAction === 'interacting' && char.action !== 'interacting' && prevInteractTarget, then calls objectEngine.release(). This SHOULD work but QA shows it doesn't reliably fire.

Possible causes:

1. The prevAction/prevInteractTarget capture (line 67-68) and char.update() (line 105) have a race — if something else changes the action between capture and check
2. arrive() is called with [char.name](<http://char.name>) but release() might use a different identifier
3. \_handleObjectArrival clears targetObjectId before startInteraction — ordering issue with the detection
4. The object's auto_next timer might reset the state (and clear the interaction) BEFORE the character's interactTimer expires, breaking the detection

## Fix

Add defensive release: when a character transitions out of interacting (in Character.js or Engine.js), always release the slot. Add console.warn for any release on an unknown occupant to catch orphaned slots.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Objects return to correct free count after character finishes interaction
* No stale full pips after 10+ sim hours

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- **`src/AIEngine.js`** — Guard `_applyDecision` to return early when `character.action` is `'interacting'` or `'talking'`. Prevents stale async LLM responses (resolved between rAF frames) from calling `moveTo()` and silently overriding the interacting state, which caused the occupancy slot to never be released.
- **`src/Engine.js`** — Added defensive release at the top of the character loop: if `interactTarget` is set but `action !== 'interacting'`, log a warning and release the slot immediately (safety net for any future path that changes action without going through normal end-of-interaction detection). Also fixed `_startConversation` to release CharB's pending object reservation before entering the talking state (`startTalk()` was clearing `targetObjectId` without calling `objectEngine.release()`).
- **`src/objects.js`** — Added `console.warn` to `ObjectInstance.release()` when the charName is not a known occupant or reservation, making any orphaned slots visible in the debug console.

## Key Decisions
- **Async guard in `_applyDecision` rather than `moveTo`**: Guarding at the decision-application level is more explicit about intent — stale decisions are intentionally discarded, not silently swallowed. The held state still releases normally via its own timer.
- **Defensive `interactTarget` check before `prevAction` capture**: Placed first in the character loop so it runs before anything else can use the stale state, and before `prevInteractTarget` is captured (ensuring the defensive release doesn't also trigger the detection below).

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/frg-229-fix-stale-slot-reservations`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-phaser/pull/13
